content.problemStatement: Danya gave integers aaa, bbb and nnn to Archi. Archi wants to compare ana^nan and bnb^nbn. Help Archi with this task.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers a, b and n.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output a single line containing:
1 if a^n > b^n,
2 if a^n < b^n,
0 if a^n = b^n.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: n is odd, compare 3 and 4 → 3 < 4 → output 2.\nTest case 2: n is even, compare |‑3|=3 and |2|=2 → 3 > 2 → output 1.
n is even, compare |5| and |‑5|, both are 5 → equal → output 0.
n is odd, compare -2 and -3 → -2 > -3 → output 1.

solution.approach: The value of a^n depends only on the parity of n. If n is odd, the sign of a is preserved and the function f(x)=x^n is strictly increasing, so a^n > b^n ⇔ a > b. If n is even, both a^n and b^n are non‑negative and f(x)=|x|^n is increasing with |x|, so a^n > b^n ⇔ |a| > |b|. Therefore we never need to compute the huge power; we only compare a and b (or their absolute values) based on n%2. The algorithm for each test case: read a, b, n; if n is even compare |a| and |b|, else compare a and b; output 1, 2 or 0 accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.
